2026-04-22T10:20:14.0371059Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0371595Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0372018Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0372388Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0372694Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0372926Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0373085Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0373264Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0373436Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0373662Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-22T10:20:14.0374146Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T10:20:14.0374415Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. This is a significant milestone: the package is now generally available with a rich API surface covering Hubs, Profiles, Interactions, KPIs, Connectors, Predictions, RelationshipLinks, Views, Widgets, and more.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Key concepts section is entirely generic** — it links only to `(azure.github.io/redacted) with no description of CustomerInsights-specific resources such as `HubResource`, `ProfileResourceFormat`, `InteractionResourceFormat`, `KpiResourceFormat`, `ConnectorResourceFormat`, `PredictionResourceFormat`, `RelationshipResourceFormat`, `RelationshipLinkResourceFormat`, `ViewResourceFormat`, and `WidgetTypeResourceFormat`.\\n- **Examples section has no actual code** — it points only to a generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` link without any CustomerInsights-specific snippets.\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` files** — there are no hand-written snippet-backed samples in `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/`; only auto-generated samples exist in `tests/Generated/Samples/`.\\n- **No README mention that this is now a stable GA release** — the package description still reads like a preview. The Getting started section should note that the package is now stable and that `--prerelease` is no longer needed.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md`:\\n\\n1. Expand the `## Key concepts` section to describe the main CustomerInsights resource hierarchy:\\n   - `HubResource` — the top-level CustomerInsights workspace resource created under a resource group\\n   - `ProfileResourceFormat` — represents a customer profile type within a hub\\n   - `InteractionResourceFormat` — represents an interaction type (customer activity) within a hub\\n   - `KpiResourceFormat` — represents a Key Performance Indicator defined against a profile or interaction\\n   - `ConnectorResourceFormat` / `ConnectorMappingResourceFormat` — data source connectors and their field mappings\\n   - `PredictionResourceFormat` — machine-learning–based predictive models within a hub\\n   - `RelationshipResourceFormat` / `RelationshipLinkResourceFormat` — semantic links between profile types\\n   - `ViewResourceFormat` — saved customer 360° views\\n   - `WidgetTypeResourceFormat` — UI widget types available for dashboards\\n\\n2. Update the `## Examples` section to reference real snippet placeholders (see Step 2 below) instead of just the generic link.\\n\\n3. Optionally add a short note under `## Getting started \\u0026gt; Install the package` confirming this is now a stable (GA) release.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new hand-written sample file at:\\n\\n**`sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample_QuickStart.cs`**\\n\\nInclude at minimum these two snippets (the generated sample files in `tests/Generated/Samples/Sample_HubCollection.cs` and `Sample_ProfileResourceFormatCollection.cs` show the right patterns to follow):\\n\\n#### Snippet 1 — Create a Hub\\n\\n```csharp\\n//  is NOT used here; this is a hand-written sample\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class Sample_QuickStart\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateHub()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateCustomerInsightsHub\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier rgId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource rg = client.GetResourceGroupResource(rgId);\\n\\n            HubCollection hubs = rg.GetHubs();\\n            HubData hubData = new HubData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;West US\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                HubBillingInfo = new HubBillingInfoFormat\\n                {\\n                    SkuName = \\u0026#34;B0\\u0026#34;,\\n                    MinUnits = 1,\\n                    MaxUnits = 5,\\n                },\\n            };\\n            ArmOperation lro = await hubs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myHub\\u0026#34;, hubData);\\n            HubResource hub = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created hub: {hub.Data.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd the following snippet placeholder to `README.md` under `## Examples`:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Create a Customer Insights Hub\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CreateCustomerInsightsHub\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README This is a significant milestone: the package is now generally available with a rich API surface covering Hubs, Profiles, Interactions, KPIs, Connectors, Predictions, RelationshipLinks, Views, Widgets, and more.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Key concepts section is entirely generic** — it links only to `(azure.github.io/redacted) with no description of CustomerInsights-specific resources such as `HubResource`, `ProfileResourceFormat`, `InteractionResourceFormat`, `KpiResourceFormat`, `ConnectorResourceFormat`, `PredictionResourceFormat`, `RelationshipResourceFormat`, `RelationshipLinkResourceFormat`, `ViewResourceFormat`, and `WidgetTypeResourceFormat`.\\n- **Examples section has no actual code** — it points only to a generic `.NET Management Library Code Samples` link without any CustomerInsights-specific snippets.\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` files** — there are no hand-written snippet-backed samples in `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/`; only auto-generated samples exist in `tests/Generated/Samples/`.\\n- **No README mention that this is now a stable GA release** — the package description still reads like a preview. The Getting started section should note that the package is now stable and that `--prerelease` is no longer needed.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md`:\\n\\n1. Expand the `## Key concepts` section to describe the main CustomerInsights resource hierarchy:\\n   - `HubResource` — the top-level CustomerInsights workspace resource created under a resource group\\n   - `ProfileResourceFormat` — represents a customer profile type within a hub\\n   - `InteractionResourceFormat` — represents an interaction type (customer activity) within a hub\\n   - `KpiResourceFormat` — represents a Key Performance Indicator defined against a profile or interaction\\n   - `ConnectorResourceFormat` / `ConnectorMappingResourceFormat` — data source connectors and their field mappings\\n   - `PredictionResourceFormat` — machine-learning–based predictive models within a hub\\n   - `RelationshipResourceFormat` / `RelationshipLinkResourceFormat` — semantic links between profile types\\n   - `ViewResourceFormat` — saved customer 360° views\\n   - `WidgetTypeResourceFormat` — UI widget types available for dashboards\\n\\n2. Update the `## Examples` section to reference real snippet placeholders (see Step 2 below) instead of just the generic link.\\n\\n3. Optionally add a short note under `## Getting started \\u0026gt; Install the package` confirming this is now a stable (GA) release.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new hand-written sample file at:\\n\\n**`sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/tests/Samples/Sample_QuickStart.cs`**\\n\\nInclude at minimum these two snippets (the generated sample files in `tests/Generated/Samples/Sample_HubCollection.cs` and `Sample_ProfileResourceFormatCollection.cs` show the right patterns to follow):\\n\\n#### Snippet 1 — Create a Hub\\n\\n```csharp\\n//  is NOT used here; this is a hand-written sample\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class Sample_QuickStart\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateHub()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateCustomerInsightsHub\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceIdentifier rgId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName);\\n            ResourceGroupResource rg = client.GetResourceGroupResource(rgId);\\n\\n            HubCollection hubs = rg.GetHubs();\\n            HubData hubData = new HubData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;West US\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                HubBillingInfo = new HubBillingInfoFormat\\n                {\\n                    SkuName = \\u0026#34;B0\\u0026#34;,\\n                    MinUnits = 1,\\n                    MaxUnits = 5,\\n                },\\n            };\\n            ArmOperation lro = await hubs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myHub\\u0026#34;, hubData);\\n            HubResource hub = lro.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created hub: {hub.Data.Id}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd the following snippet placeholder to `README.md` under `## Examples`:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Create a Customer Insights Hub\\n\\n```C# Snippet:CreateCustomerInsightsHub\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/README.md` currently has all required top-level sections (`Getting started`, `Key concepts`, `Documentation`, `Examples`, `Troubleshooting`, `Next steps`, `Contributing`). The following sections need content updates:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action |\\n|---------|--------|--------|\\n| Key concepts | ❌ Generic only | Replace generic link with CustomerInsights resource hierarchy description |\\n| Examples | ❌ Generic link only | Add snippet placeholders for Hub creation and profile listing |\\n| Getting started | ⚠️ No stable-release note | Optionally add \\u0026#34;This package is now generally available\\u0026#34; sentence |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 customer-insights` — injects snippet code into README.md placeholder blocks; verify the README diff is correct\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 customer-insights` — regenerates public API listing (required if any public types were added)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/customer-insights/Azure.ResourceManager.CustomerInsights/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
